confirmed.issue: Prunus subhirtella 'Autumnalis' (Autumn/Winter Flowering Cherry) is named and grown for its autumn-into-winter flowering flush (roughly Nov-Mar) plus a spring display. A bloom window of Mar-Apr only shows the spring flush and omits the defining autumn bloom, contradicting the plant's common name and hiding the trait people plant it for.
confirmed.expected: A bloom window including the autumn/winter period (e.g. Nov-Mar) alongside the spring flush
confirmed.verifyReason: RHS and Missouri Botanical Garden confirm semi-double pale flowers open in flushes during mild spells from ~November through March. 'Mar-Apr' captures only the tail-end spring flush. Not a defensible typical-approximation; it contradicts the cultivar's identity.

confirmed.current: tags [rosa, red, english rose, fragrant, repeat flowering]; bloom colour #f5f0e1 (cream)
confirmed.issue: David Austin's 'Patience' (Auspastor) is a soft blush/cream-to-apricot rose, correctly reflected by its cream hex #f5f0e1, so the 'red' colour search tag clearly contradicts the actual bloom colour and would misfile it in colour searches.
confirmed.verifyReason: David Austin's 'Patience' (2013) is a creamy-white English rose opening from apricot-tinged buds, strongly fragrant and repeat-flowering. It is not a red rose. The 'red' tag conflicts with the record's own cream hex and misfiles it in colour searches.
